The '''IG-97 battle droid''' was a model of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] constructed early in the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], also called '''Imperial Battle Droids''' by some.

==History==
[[Phlut Design Systems/Legends|Phlut Design Systems]], who had constructed [[IG lancer droid/Legends|IG lancer droids]] for the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]], was bought by [[Holowan Mechanicals/Legends|Holowan Mechanicals]] which also acquired and modified the plans to the original lancers. Holowan thus produced the IG-97, along with the [[IG-88 assassin droid/Legends|IG-88]] series, [[IG-72]], and the [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuard]] series.

Similar in design to the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] employed by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Empire realized they were too weak to perform in battle, thus cutting production.

The Imperial officer [[Rom Mohc]] once defeated an IG-97 prototype in man-to-droid combat and kept it as a trophy.

[[Iggy|One particular IG-97]] unit rose to prominence on Tatooine, where he helped [[Gizman|Big Gizz]], [[Spiker]], [[Onoh]], and [[Klepti B'ay]] defeat a rogue [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark trooper]].
